content_of_post_to_answer: How do I accept something that will never happen?
answer: Accepting that something will never happen is not the same as giving up on it. Often the hardest part is not losing the future we wanted, but losing the identity we built around that future. A useful shift is to ask: “What was this hope trying to give me?” Sometimes we are not attached to the event itself, but to the meaning we expected from it. Acceptance begins when we stop negotiating with reality and start carrying that meaning forward in another form.
